T2.DMM 0.1.0

  • Articles
    • Getting Started with the Concept Management Model Package

    Delete Duplicate Rows in CDM Table of Origin Table

    delete_duplicates_origin.Rd

    This function removes duplicate rows from specified CDM tables based on a given set of columns. Duplicates are identified using `rowid`, keeping the first occurrence and deleting the rest.

    delete_duplicates_origin(
  db_connection,
  scheme,
  save_deleted = FALSE,
  save_path = NULL,
  add_postfix = NA,
  schema_name = NULL,
  to_view = FALSE,
  pipeline_extension = "_T2DMM"
)

    Arguments

    db_connection

    Database connection object (SQLiteConnection or DuckDB connection).

    scheme

    Named list where names are CDM table names and values are character vectors of columns to use for uniqueness. Use `"*"` to indicate all columns.

    save_deleted

    Logical. If TRUE, deleted rows are saved as CSV files. Default is FALSE.

    save_path

    Character. Directory path where deleted records are saved. Mandatory when `save_deleted = TRUE`.

    add_postfix

    Optional character string. If provided, adds a postfix to the saved CSV file names. Default is `NA`.

    schema_name

    Character. Name of the schema where tables are located. Default is `"main"`.

    to_view

    Logical. If TRUE, instead of deleting rows, a view is created with distinct rows based on the specified columns. Default is FALSE.

    pipeline_extension

    When using views when applying the clean_missing_values on CDM table we must define the name of the pipeline extension. See add_view for more information.

    Details

    The function can: - Directly delete duplicates from the database. - Optionally save deleted records as CSV files for tracking. - Optionally create a view instead of deleting, keeping only distinct rows.

    Examples
